summary: Phase 3B end-to-end testing complete. Backend: 22/22 NEW pytest cases PASS in /app/backend/tests/test_resumes_and_matches.py covering resume upload (401/415/400/201), .txt parsing extracts Python/SQL/Selenium/Pandas/FastAPI skills, raw text stored in separate resume_texts collection (raw_text_chars > 100), second upload toggles is_active correctly, GET /resumes /resumes/active /activate /delete work as specified, first-resume autosaves profile.skills, opportunities/meta returns freshness_windows=['24h','3d','7d','30d'], invalid '99h' returns 422, freshness=24h filters by date_posted/date_found, new nullable fields (date_posted/last_verified/freshness_score) present in responses, GET /matches/opportunity returns null when no resume and full MatchResult with breakdown (matched_skills, missing_skills, extra_skills, skill_score, role_relevance + explanation, experience_relevance + explanation, location_relevance + explanation) when resume exists, ?tfidf=true populates tfidf_score and is cached, /matches/batch returns BatchMatchItem rows with matched_count/missing_count, cross-user GET returns 404, deleting active resume invalidates cached matches, REAL Claude calls to POST /resumes/{id}/enrich and POST /matches/opportunity/{id}/ai both work AND a SECOND call returns identical ai_enriched_at (cached, no new LLM call). Phase 3A regression: 25/25 still PASS. Frontend Playwright: register, profile dialog, resume-section + dropzone, resume-file-input upload, resume-item-<id>, resume-skills-<id> chips, resume-enrich-btn-<id>, freshness select with 24h option filters list, are ALL working. CRITICAL UI GAP: the Match column does NOT render the percentage badges because (a) the <thead> in OpportunitiesTable.jsx lists only 8 columns (Match column header missing) while <tbody> renders 9 <td> cells, mis-aligning columns; and (b) OpportunitiesView.jsx never calls resumesApi.active() or matchesApi.batch(), so hasResume stays false and matchSummary stays {} — every row falls back to <match-badge-no-resume-id> dash. As a result the spec-required match-badge-<id>, match-details-<id>, match-tfidf-btn-<id>, match-ai-btn-<id> selectors are unreachable through normal flow.

ui_bugs.issue: <thead> defines 8 column headers but <tbody> renders 9 <td> cells (MatchBadge cell inserted after Role/Company at line 204). Add a 'Match' entry to the header array (line 152-160) right after 'Role / Company' so columns align AND the Match column has a visible label.

hasResume is initialised to false (line 42) and matchSummary to {} (line 41) but NEITHER is ever updated. There is no call to resumesApi.active() to set hasResume and no call to matchesApi.batch(opportunity_ids) to populate matchSummary. Result: every row renders match-badge-no-resume-<id> instead of match-badge-<id>, breaking the entire match-score UX (badge, popover, TF-IDF compute, AI insight).

FIXED BY TESTING AGENT - file previously had 18 lines of duplicated/orphan JSX after the component closing `};` at line 168 (SyntaxError: Unexpected token at line 169:21), which prevented the frontend from compiling at all (Compiled with problems: Module build failed). Removed the orphan block; webpack rebuilt cleanly. Main agent should review the merge that introduced the duplication.

integration_issues.issue: Backend /api/matches/batch + /api/resumes/active are correct and return data, but the frontend never invokes them, so the integration is broken on the UI side only.

issues: Because Match column header is missing, the table is misaligned: TYPE column shows the MatchBadge dash above the actual Internship/Hybrid text; all subsequent columns are visually offset by one cell.

action_items: OpportunitiesView.jsx: add useEffect that calls resumesApi.active() to set hasResume(!!resume), and another useEffect (triggered when opps changes) that calls matchesApi.batch({opportunity_ids: opps.map(o=>o.id)}) and maps the result into matchSummary keyed by opportunity_id ({overall_score, jaccard_score, skill_score, matched_count, missing_count}). Trigger refetch after a successful resume upload (e.g., listen for an onResumeChanged event from ProfileDialog or call from handleSeed/handleChanged).
OpportunitiesTable.jsx (line ~152): insert 'Match' into the header array so the 9 body cells line up with 9 header cells.
Code review: there was orphan JSX after the component close in OpportunitiesView.jsx that the testing agent removed to unblock the build. Inspect the git diff to ensure no real code was lost.
critical_code_review_comments: OpportunitiesView.jsx maintains matchSummary + hasResume state but never fetches them — dead code path leading to broken UX. Add the two missing fetch effects.
OpportunitiesTable.jsx hard-codes column count in <thead>; consider deriving headers from a single source array to avoid future mismatches.

matches.py _compute caches but the cache key includes only (user_id, resume_id, opportunity_id) — when profile.preferred_roles/locations changes the cached score is stale. Consider profile.updated_at in the cache key OR invalidate match_results on profile PUT (low priority).
resumes.py upload deletes ALL match_results for the user on every upload (line 107) — heavy when batch matching has been done. Could scope to the previous active resume only (low priority perf).
ResumePublic does not expose ai_summary directly — only via parsed.ai_summary. ResumeSection.jsx uses parsed.ai_summary so it works, but consider promoting for clarity.
server.py still uses deprecated @app.on_event hooks (carried over). Migrate to lifespan handler.
Both LLM routes parse JSON with greedy regex `\{.*\}` which can fail if the model wraps in code fences. Consider stripping ```json fences first.
updated_files: /app/frontend/src/components/lead/OpportunitiesView.jsx (removed 18 lines of orphan duplicate JSX after component close that was breaking the webpack build)

success_rate.frontend: 75% — resume upload + parsing + freshness filter work; Match column missing/misaligned and matchSummary never fetched so AI/TFIDF UI is unreachable

test_credentials: Demo: demo@leadfinder.app / Demo1234!. TEST_ prefixed users were created during the run and are kept (TEST_3b_a_*, TEST_3b_b_*, TEST_3bui_*) — safe to leave or purge.
seed_data_creation: Each TEST_ user calls POST /api/opportunities/seed to get the 5 sample opportunities; uploads a small generated .txt resume containing Python/SQL/Selenium/Pandas/FastAPI to validate skill mining. Object storage uploads completed in 1-3s as expected.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 22 backend tests in test_resumes_and_matches.py + 25 regression tests in test_auth_and_isolation.py PASS (47/47). The only outstanding work is the frontend Match-column wiring in OpportunitiesView.jsx — backend endpoints (/api/resumes/active, /api/matches/batch, /api/matches/opportunity/{id}, /api/matches/opportunity/{id}/ai) all verified working via pytest. Once OpportunitiesView fetches active resume + batch matches and pushes them into the table, the badge/popover/TFIDF/AI flow should immediately light up because MatchBadge.jsx, all match-* data-testids, and the API client are already correct.
rca of the issue: (1) Webpack build failure: OpportunitiesView.jsx had duplicate JSX pasted after the component's closing `};` (lines 169-186), producing 'SyntaxError: Unexpected token (169:21)' which made the frontend serve only the CRA error overlay. Removed the duplicate block — restored clean build. (2) Match column unreachable: OpportunitiesView.jsx declares matchSummary/hasResume state but has no useEffect to populate them. resumesApi and matchesApi exist in /app/frontend/src/lib/api.js and the backend endpoints are validated via pytest. Reproduction: register a new user, upload a .txt resume in Profile, go to Find Opportunities, seed sample data — every row shows a `—` placeholder rendered by `match-badge-no-resume-<opp_id>` rather than the percentage badge. (3) Header mismatch: <thead> header array has 8 entries but each <tr> has 9 <td>, so the Match column has no label and visually pushes columns out of alignment. Fix actions are listed under action_items.
